meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve no testing knowledge (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no testing knowledge. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Project Template for Testing
content.seo_description: Master professional testing workflows. Use this Antigravity project template and Claude Code to eliminate testing gaps in your student portfolio projects.
content.answer_block: Testing is the invisible wall between a student script and a production-ready SaaS. By integrating the Antigravity project template, you automate test scaffolding, ensuring every function is validated. Using Claude Code to orchestrate these tests transforms your workflow from manual debugging to robust, automated software engineering excellence.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting the perfect logic for your first SaaS, but the moment you deploy, the house of cards collapses. The silence of your console hides critical bugs that only surface when a user touches your interface. This is the 'Testing Void'—the primary reason student projects fail to transition into career-defining portfolio pieces.

But what if you could bake professional-grade reliability into your architecture before writing a single line of feature code? By leveraging the Antigravity project template, you gain a rigorous, automated testing harness that forces your code to be modular, predictable, and indestructible. It is time to stop guessing if your app works and start proving it.

steps.title: Initialize with Antigravity Template
steps.description: Scaffold your project with the Antigravity template to ensure structural integrity and pre-configured test runners.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new project structure using the Antigravity standard template, ensuring Jest and Supertest are pre-configured for a Node.js environment.

steps.title: Generate Unit Tests with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to analyze your core business logic and generate comprehensive unit tests covering edge cases.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the src/services/auth.ts file and generate a corresponding test suite that handles successful logins, invalid credentials, and rate-limiting scenarios.

steps.title: Implement Integration Hooks
steps.description: Link your database and API layers to the Antigravity test lifecycle to simulate real-world data flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the test environment to use an ephemeral SQLite database for integration tests, ensuring no state leakage between test runs.

steps.title: Automate Regression Testing
steps.description: Create a watch-mode loop where every save triggers a selective test run to catch regressions instantly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a file-watcher script that triggers the Antigravity test suite specifically for modified modules to minimize build times.

steps.title: Refactor for Testability
steps.description: Use Claude Code to identify tightly coupled code and refactor it into testable, dependency-injected modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Identify functions in the controller layer that are difficult to test due to side effects and refactor them using dependency injection.

pro_tips: Always mock external API calls to avoid hitting rate limits and incurring costs during your development cycle.
Aim for 'Branch Coverage' rather than 'Line Coverage' to ensure your logic paths are actually being tested.
Use Claude Code to generate 'Negative Test Cases'—force your app to break with bad data to see how it handles errors.
Treat your test suite as documentation; if a developer can't understand the feature by reading the test, the test is too complex.
Run your test suite as part of a pre-commit hook to ensure zero 'broken' code ever enters your repository.
content.next_steps: Once your tests are passing, integrate a CI/CD pipeline like GitHub Actions to automatically run your Antigravity test suite on every push to your main branch.
content.conversion_block: Stop building apps that break. Start building systems that last. You have the tools, the template, and the roadmap. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
